martini.note: Sean Connery's James Bond first orders a vodka martini, "shaken, not stirred."
Bond famously specifies his martini preferences while held captive.
Bette Davis holds court with a martini in the iconic party scenes.
Nick and Nora Charles make martini drinking look effortlessly glamorous throughout.
Martinis appear throughout as the detectives navigate 1950s Los Angeles.

vodka-martini.note: Daniel Craig's Bond orders his signature drink with specific instructions.
Pierce Brosnan's first Bond film features the classic "shaken, not stirred" line.
Bond is served a martini on Silva's island, noting it is "perfect."
Roger Moore's Bond orders his trademark vodka martini aboard the submarine.

dirty-martini.note: Bond's final outing includes his signature martini, this time with olive brine.
Vince Vaughn and Jon Favreau navigate LA's cocktail lounges, ordering dirty martinis.

vesper.note: Bond invents the Vesper, naming it after Vesper Lynd, specifying Gordon's gin, vodka, and Kina Lillet.

gibson.note: The sophisticated Gibson appears among the classic cocktails served at Margo Channing's party.

white-russian.note: The Dude drinks White Russians throughout the entire film, making it the cocktail's most famous cultural moment.
Brad Pitt's character orders a White Russian at a bar.

black-russian.note: Before switching to White Russians, The Dude is seen with the simpler Black Russian.

cosmopolitan.note: Carrie Bradshaw and friends drink Cosmos throughout, continuing the TV series tradition.
The iconic foursome toast with Cosmopolitans in Abu Dhabi.
The Cosmo features as the stylish drink of choice at the local bar.

manhattan.note: Manhattans flow freely at the party on the train, made in a hot water bottle.
Burt Lancaster's J.J. Hunsecker drinks Manhattans in the jazz clubs of New York.
Jack Lemmon's character prepares Manhattans in his borrowed apartment.
The cocktail's namesake city provides the backdrop for this romantic comedy.

old-fashioned.note: George Bailey orders a series of Old Fashioneds at Martini's bar.
Ryan Gosling's character teaches Steve Carell to order an Old Fashioned instead of a vodka cranberry.
Officer K drinks an Old Fashioned at a bar while investigating.
Wall Street power players sip Old Fashioneds while the financial world crumbles around them.
Ben Affleck's meticulous character orders a precise Old Fashioned.

margarita.note: Tom Cruise's bartender character makes and serves Margaritas in several scenes.
Antonio Banderas drinks in a Mexican bar where Margaritas are prominently featured.
Jim Carrey and Téa Leoni's characters enjoy Margaritas during their suburban life.
The Tex-Mex border setting features Margaritas in the bar scenes.

daiquiri.note: Alec Guinness drinks Daiquiris in pre-revolution Havana, echoing Hemingway's famous habit.
A Daiquiri is ordered at a Havana bar in Michael Mann's stylish crime thriller.

frozen-daiquiri.note: The film depicts Hemingway's love of the double-frozen Daiquiri at El Floridita bar in Havana.
Johnny Depp's journalist character drinks frozen Daiquiris in 1960s Puerto Rico.

hemingway-daiquiri.note: The film depicts Hemingway's love of the double-frozen Daiquiri at El Floridita bar.

mojito.note: James Bond orders a Mojito in Havana, introducing the cocktail to a new generation.
Colin Farrell's Crockett drinks Mojitos in the sultry Miami nightlife scenes.
The Cuban classic appears alongside the Daiquiri in this Graham Greene adaptation.

champagne-cocktail.note: Champagne cocktails are served at Rick's Café Américain throughout the film.
Champagne flows at the party where Ingrid Bergman's character gathers intelligence.
Champagne cocktails are consumed at Gatsby's extravagant parties.
First-class passengers enjoy champagne cocktails in the ship's dining rooms.

gimlet.note: Elliott Gould's Philip Marlowe orders gimlets, echoing Chandler's novel: "a real gimlet is half gin and half Rose's Lime Juice and nothing else."
Humphrey Bogart's Marlowe drinks gimlets in this classic noir.

singapore-sling.movie: Fear and Loathing in Las Vegas
singapore-sling.note: Johnny Depp's Raoul Duke orders Singapore Slings in the hotel bar, calling it "the only way to fly."

mai-tai.note: Elvis Presley sips Mai Tais in this tropical musical, helping popularize tiki culture.
Mai Tais flow freely at the Hawaiian resort where Jason Segel's character tries to move on.
The characters discuss doing nothing and drinking Mai Tais as the ultimate fantasy.

pina-colada.note: Tom Cruise blends Piña Coladas in his flashy bartending routines.
Tom Cruise's character enjoys Piña Coladas during his Cayman Islands trip.
The Hawaiian resort setting features Piña Coladas as the quintessential vacation drink.
Piña Coladas are served during the uncomfortable family gathering scenes.

gin-and-tonic.note: Katharine Hepburn's character disapproves of Humphrey Bogart's gin, though the spirit is central to the plot.
Robert Redford and Meryl Streep share gin and tonics on the Kenyan savannah.
The British retirees carry on the colonial tradition of G&Ts in India.
Gin and tonics flow at the aristocratic country estate.

mint-julep.note: Tom Buchanan serves Mint Juleps on a sweltering summer afternoon, a pivotal scene.
Robert Redford's Gatsby features the same iconic Mint Julep scene from the novel.
Doc Holliday orders a Mint Julep, staying true to his Southern roots.
Mint Juleps are served trackside at the Kentucky Derby scenes.

french-75.note: French 75s are served at Rick's Café, ordered by the stylish clientele.
Russell Crowe's character enjoys French 75s in the south of France.

screwdriver.note: Holly Golightly's party scenes feature Screwdrivers among the flowing cocktails.
Oskar Schindler drinks Screwdrivers while entertaining Nazi officers.

bloody-mary.note: The classic morning-after remedy is discussed as the group pieces together their lost night.
Bridget reaches for Bloody Marys as her go-to hangover cure.
Tom Cruise's bartender whips up Bloody Marys during his training montage.
The pub crawl crew encounters the cocktail during their epic drinking marathon.

cuba-libre.note: Cuba Libres are served throughout, set in 1958 pre-revolution Havana.
Rum and Coke flows in the Havana scenes as Michael Corleone expands operations to Cuba.

rum-and-coke.note: The simple rum and Coke is the drink of choice in the Havana nightlife scenes.
The fraternity party staple features prominently in the toga party scenes.

whiskey-sour.note: Whiskey Sours are mixed and consumed during the memorable party scenes.
Humphrey Bogart's screenwriter character drinks Whiskey Sours at his local bar.
Ray Milland's alcoholic writer orders Whiskey Sours in Billy Wilder's harrowing drama.

tequila-sunrise.note: The cocktail is the namesake of this crime thriller starring Mel Gibson and Michelle Pfeiffer.

sidecar.note: The Sidecar is featured as a classic Parisian cocktail in the animated film's bar scene.
The Sidecar appears in the post-WWI Parisian setting, reflecting its era of invention.

negroni.note: Negronis are sipped in the Italian café scenes, fitting the film's Mediterranean setting.
Ryan Gosling's suave character introduces proper cocktails, including the Negroni.
Woody Allen's Roman comedy features Negronis as part of the Italian aperitivo tradition.

boulevardier.note: The 1920s Paris setting evokes the era when Erskine Gwynne, an American boulevardier, created this cocktail.

grasshopper.note: Quentin Tarantino features the retro Grasshopper in the bar scenes of this grindhouse thriller.

long-island-iced-tea.note: Simon Pegg's character attempts an epic pub crawl where strong drinks like Long Islands feature.
Katherine Heigl's character drinks Long Island Iced Teas at the bar.
The rowdy bar scenes feature Long Island Iced Teas as a popular order.

brandy-alexander.movie: The Days of Wine and Roses
brandy-alexander.note: Jack Lemmon introduces Lee Remick to drinking with a Brandy Alexander, starting her spiral.
Peter Sellers orders Brandy Alexanders in this comedy.

irish-coffee.note: John Huston's adaptation of Joyce's story features Irish Coffee at the holiday dinner party.
Irish Coffee warms the characters in this charming Irish comedy.

stinger.note: While not the film's namesake, Stingers are consumed in the elegant 1930s setting.
Bing Crosby and Grace Kelly's characters enjoy Stingers at the lavish Newport party.

rob-roy.note: While the film is about the Scottish folk hero, the cocktail named after him gains renewed attention.

bellini.note: Johnny Depp and Angelina Jolie drink Bellinis at Harry's Bar in Venice, where the cocktail was invented.
The Venice setting evokes the Bellini's birthplace at Harry's Bar.

rusty-nail.note: Michael Caine's Harry Palmer drinks Rusty Nails in this British spy thriller.

sazerac.note: The New Orleans setting features the city's signature cocktail in the bar scenes.

sazerac.movie: The Curious Case of Benjamin Button
sazerac.note: Sazeracs appear in the New Orleans-set story spanning decades.
The antebellum Louisiana setting features the cocktail born in New Orleans.

tom-collins.note: William Powell's Nick Charles orders Tom Collins alongside his many other cocktails.

jack-and-coke.note: The high schoolers aspire to drink Jack and Coke at the party.
Joaquin Phoenix's Johnny Cash is frequently seen with whiskey and Coke.

sex-on-the-beach.note: The beach-bar cocktail culture of the 1980s is on full display in this Tom Cruise classic.
The party cocktail is among the drinks served at the raucous bar.

hurricane.note: New Orleans' signature Hurricane cocktail appears in the Bourbon Street scenes.
The French Quarter setting evokes the cocktail culture of New Orleans.

death-in-the-afternoon.note: Owen Wilson's character meets Hemingway in 1920s Paris, where the writer's famous absinthe-champagne creation would have been born.

absinthe-frappe.note: Absinthe features prominently with the Green Fairy sequence in Baz Luhrmann's musical.
Johnny Depp's Inspector Abberline is an absinthe drinker in this Victorian-era thriller.
Absinthe is consumed in Coppola's Gothic horror, fitting the decadent Victorian era.
Jude Law's character enjoys absinthe cocktails in his London nightlife adventures.

americano.note: In Ian Fleming's first Bond novel, 007 drinks an Americano, not a martini.

harvey-wallbanger.note: The quintessential 1970s cocktail appears in Warren Beatty's satirical comedy of the era.

kir-royale.note: Edina and Patsy drink Kir Royales as part of their champagne-fueled lifestyle.
Kir Royales are served at the aristocratic English country house gathering.

aperol-spritz.note: Diane Lane's character embraces Italian life, with Aperol Spritzes as part of the aperitivo culture.
The Italian aperitivo tradition, including Spritzes, features in Woody Allen's Roman comedy.
The Venetian spritz is omnipresent in the film's Venice canal-side café scenes.

godfather.note: While the cocktail is named after the film, Scotch and amaretto are consumed in the iconic saga.

godmother.note: The vodka-amaretto variation of the Godfather cocktail echoes the saga's continuation.

espresso-martini.note: The Espresso Martini makes appearances as a trendy cocktail choice.

zombie.note: While the film is about actual zombies, the characters end up at a pub where strong cocktails flow.

eggnog.note: Chevy Chase's Clark Griswold drinks eggnog from a moose-shaped glass in the classic holiday comedy.
Eggnog appears during the Christmas celebrations in this romantic comedy.
Will Ferrell's Buddy pours eggnog with his spaghetti breakfast in a memorable scene.

hot-toddy.note: The warming Hot Toddy is among the comfort drinks consumed during Bridget's winter misadventures.
Colin Farrell and Brendan Gleeson warm up with hot drinks in the wintry Belgian city.

hot-buttered-rum.note: Bing Crosby and Danny Kaye's characters enjoy warming cocktails at the Vermont lodge.

mulled-wine.note: Mulled wine is served at the school Christmas concert and holiday gatherings.
Kate Winslet's character embraces English winter with mulled wine.

mulled-wine.movie: Harry Potter and the Half-Blood Prince
mulled-wine.note: Mulled mead is served at The Three Broomsticks, the wizarding take on mulled wine.

moscow-mule.note: Tony Stark's extravagant party lifestyle includes Moscow Mules in their signature copper mugs.

dark-n-stormy.note: The rum-and-ginger cocktail's stormy name fits the film's tempestuous Atlantic setting.

aviation.note: Leonardo DiCaprio's Howard Hughes era evokes the golden age when the Aviation cocktail was created.

pisco-sour.note: The eclectic Tenenbaum family's cocktail selections include South American classics.

caipirinha.note: Brazil's national cocktail is part of the vibrant Rio de Janeiro backdrop.
The crew celebrates in Rio with Caipirinhas after their heist.

paloma.note: The Mexican border setting features the Paloma as the country's most popular tequila cocktail.

bees-knees.note: The cocktail's Prohibition-era origins perfectly match Gatsby's extravagant 1920s parties.
Prohibition-era bootleggers serve honey-sweetened cocktails to mask bathtub gin.

corpse-reviver-no-2.note: Nick Charles's hangover cures evoke the Corpse Reviver tradition of morning-after cocktails.

gin-fizz.note: Nick and Nora Charles drink their way through Prohibition-era New York, with fizzes among their repertoire.
Rosalind Russell's flamboyant Mame serves gin fizzes at her legendary parties.

ramos-gin-fizz.movie: The Curious Case of Benjamin Button
ramos-gin-fizz.note: New Orleans' legendary frothy cocktail fits the film's century-spanning NOLA setting.

blood-and-sand.note: Tyrone Power stars in this bullfighting drama, the cocktail's namesake film.
Rudolph Valentino's silent film original inspired the cocktail's creation.

between-the-sheets.note: The risqué cocktail name fits perfectly with the film's innuendo-laden humor.

blue-lagoon.note: While the film features a tropical island rather than the cocktail, the vivid blue drink became associated with its name.

b-52.note: The layered shooter appears during Tom Cruise's flamboyant bartending performances.

kamikaze.note: The sharp vodka shooter is among the drinks Tom Cruise's character masters.

mudslide.note: The creamy, indulgent cocktail shares the spirit of The Dude's beloved White Russians.

el-presidente.note: The Cuban classic fits the Havana scenes where Michael Corleone meets with the president.

mary-pickford.note: The silent-film era setting evokes the Hollywood golden age when Mary Pickford ruled the screen and inspired the cocktail.

white-lady.note: Wes Anderson's elegant 1930s European setting evokes the cocktail's Jazz Age origins.

pink-lady.note: While the film's Pink Ladies gang isn't named after the cocktail, the association became inseparable in popular culture.

martinez.note: The Martinez, the martini's ancestor, fits the film's pre-war cocktail sophistication.

french-connection.note: While the film is about drug smuggling, the cognac-amaretto cocktail shares its name.

scotch-and-soda.note: Bill Murray's aging actor drinks Scotch in the lonely Tokyo hotel bars.
Cary Grant is forced to drink bourbon on the rocks, but the Scotch highball is referenced throughout.

bourbon-and-branch.note: Clint Eastwood's retired gunslinger returns to the whiskey-soaked saloon world of the Old West.

whiskey-highball.note: Bill Murray films a Suntory whisky commercial, making the Japanese highball a cultural touchpoint.

mizuwari.note: Bill Murray's Suntory whisky scenes in Tokyo popularized Japanese whisky drinking culture worldwide.

appletini.note: The bright green Appletini epitomizes the early-2000s cocktail culture satirized in this comedy.

lemon-drop-martini.note: Reese Witherspoon's Elle Woods embodies the sweet, stylish cocktail culture of the era.

chocolate-martini.movie: Bridget Jones: The Edge of Reason
chocolate-martini.note: Bridget's indulgent cocktail choices include the chocolate martini.

pimms-cup.note: Pimm's Cup is the official drink of the tennis tournament that gives the film its name.
The quintessential English summer drink appears at the garden party.

garibaldi.note: The simple Campari-orange combination fits the Italian aperitivo scenes.

shirley-temple.note: The child passenger orders a Shirley Temple in the disaster spoof, played for laughs.
Shirley Temple herself stars in the film that helped make her namesake mocktail famous.

daisy-buchanan.note: Named after F. Scott Fitzgerald's character, brought to life by Carey Mulligan in Baz Luhrmann's adaptation.
Mia Farrow's portrayal of Daisy in the Robert Redford version.

jungle-juice.note: The notorious party punch is a staple of John Belushi's fraternity chaos.
The house party features jungle juice as the go-to communal drink.

shandy.note: The Winchester pub scenes feature the kind of casual British drinking the shandy embodies.

snowball.note: The retro advocaat cocktail fits Bridget's charmingly old-fashioned British sensibilities.

michelada.note: The Mexican beer cocktail appears in this road trip through Mexico.

carajillo.note: The coffee-liqueur cocktail is part of the Mexico City daily life depicted in Alfonso Cuarón's masterpiece.

gluhwein.note: The Central European setting evokes the tradition of warming Glühwein at mountain resorts.

ti-punch.note: The Martinican rum cocktail reflects the French Caribbean heritage explored in this Palme d'Or winner.

planters-punch.note: Bond drinks rum punch in Jamaica in his first cinematic adventure.
Johnny Depp's journalist in Puerto Rico encounters various Caribbean rum drinks.

rum-punch.note: Quentin Tarantino adapted the Elmore Leonard novel originally titled 'Rum Punch.'

rum-punch.movie: Pirates of the Caribbean: The Curse of the Black Pearl
rum-punch.note: Captain Jack Sparrow's love of rum makes punch the natural celebratory drink.

corn-n-oil.note: The Caribbean setting features traditional rum drinks including the Bajan classic.

new-york-sour.note: Martin Scorsese's gilded-age New York setting evokes the era when this cocktail was born.

vieux-carre.movie: The Curious Case of Benjamin Button
vieux-carre.note: Named after New Orleans' French Quarter, the Vieux Carré fits the film's NOLA setting.

ward-eight.note: The Boston-born cocktail fits Martin Scorsese's crime drama set in the same city.

sloe-gin-fizz.note: The fizzy cocktail appears among the drinks consumed at the high-society party.

sherry-cobbler.note: The Sherry Cobbler was the most popular American cocktail during the Civil War era depicted in the film.

brandy-crusta.note: The pre-Civil War Southern setting evokes the era when the Crusta was created in New Orleans.

fernandito.note: Argentina's national drink appears in this Buenos Aires-set thriller.
